title: Core: Search / Merge / Rank (reads cache, never live-fetches)
description:
The user-facing read path. See test-strategy Module 4 + degradation A3.

WHAT: search(query:{date|dateRange, timeWindow?, players?, holes?, courseIds?}) reading ONLY from the store: gather each course's cached slots, filter, merge into one time-sorted list, rank 'good times' (config: preferred windows), and tag each course result state healthy | stale | deep-link-only (A3: an un-scrapable/errored course is surfaced with a deep link + flag, NEVER dropped). One course's error must not sink the merge.

FILES: src/search/search.ts.
UNIT (test/search/search.unit.test.ts): merge->time-sorted; each filter (day/window/players/holes); rank ordering; overlapping identical times across courses; empty results; ALL-degraded; one-course-error isolation; stale surfaced+flagged.
INTEGRATION (test/search/search.integration.test.ts, REAL sqlite store seeded): search served entirely FROM the store with adapter-call-count asserted == 0 (the load-bearing tradeoff #4 test); 3-state seed (healthy/stale/deep-link-only) differentiated in output.
ACCEPTANCE: never-live-fetch assertion green; degradation states distinct.
VERIFY: npx vitest run test/search
acceptance_criteria: Merge time-sorted; all filters (day/window/players/holes) correct; one-course-error isolated; overlapping times + empty + all-degraded handled; INTEGRATION asserts search served from store with adapter-call-count==0; healthy/stale/deep-link-only states distinct. VERIFY: npx vitest run test/search
notes: FILTER-CAPABILITY EVIDENCE (from live captures): players (# golfers) and time filtering are BOTH natively supported per backend AND applied uniformly in this layer. Per-backend native params discovered: Tee-On SearchResults POST accepts Players(1-4) + SearchTime(single start time, ''=any) + Holes(18|9); Chronogolf teetimes accepts player count + nb_holes + date; LinkLine InitialData exposes Players[Any,-1..4] + a TIME WINDOW (AvailabilityStartTime/AvailabilityEndTime, default 06:00-20:00, TeeTimeInterval 15) + Holes[Any,18,9] + Price slider. DESIGN: '# golfers' maps to Slot.spotsAvailable — search({players:N}) keeps slots with spotsAvailable>=N; 'desired time slots' maps to search({timeWindow:{start,end}}) applied over course-local Slot.time. We filter in THIS layer for a uniform experience across all courses (pass the native param through as a fetch optimization where a backend supports it, e.g. LinkLine window / Tee-On SearchTime; window client-side where it returns all-day). Same timeWindow+players vocabulary feeds the watcher AlertRule (G2). EZLinks (deep-link-only) can't be server-filtered — surfaced as a link, filtering N/A.

title: Core: Slot model + slotKey + change-semantics + AdapterError + AvailabilityAdapter interface
description:
FOUNDATION every other bead depends on. See epic DESIGN field (Interface contracts) + test-strategy .claude/sable/charters/tee-times-z4v-test-strategy.md (Module 1) + gap resolutions in epic notes (G1).

WHAT: Define in TypeScript (zod for runtime validation):
- Slot = {courseId, backendId, date, time (course-local HH:MM), holes: 9|18, spotsAvailable:int, price?, bookingUrl, raw?}.
- slotKey(slot) = `${courseId}|${date}|${time}|${holes}` (G1: spotsAvailable is an ATTRIBUTE, NOT part of identity).
- classifyChange(prev?, curr?) -> 'NEW'|'REMOVED'|'FREED'|'FILLED'|'SAME' per G1: NEW=key added; REMOVED=key gone OR spots->0; FREED=spots increased; FILLED=spots decreased but >0.
- AdapterError {backendId, courseId, kind:'blocked'|'parse'|'network'|'auth', retryable:bool}.
- AvailabilityAdapter interface: {backendId; listSlots(courseRef, date, query:{players?,holes?}) => Promise<Slot[]>}. Invariant I1: returns normalized Slot[] OR throws AdapterError; never [] to mean broken; never backend-shaped data. I3: bookingUrl always deep-links to the course's own booking page.
- courseRef discriminated union: TeeOnRef{backend:'tee-on',courseCode,courseGroupId} | EzlinksRef{backend:'ezlinks',subdomain,facilityId} | ChronogolfRef{backend:'chronogolf',clubId,courseId,affiliationTypeId} | LinklineRef{backend:'linkline',host,courseId}.

FILES: src/core/slot.ts, src/core/adapter.ts, src/core/errors.ts.

UNIT (Vitest, test/core/slot.test.ts): slotKey stability + no collision across differing fields; classifyChange for all 5 transitions incl spots 4->2 (FILLED, still bookable) and 0->2 (FREED); Slot zod rejects bad shapes; AdapterError construction.
INTEGRATION: [no-integration] pure type/function module with no external deps — its integration coverage is delivered by every adapter/store bead that consumes these types (the interface is exercised there). Reason recorded per the unit+integration rule.

ACCEPTANCE: types compile; slotKey + classifyChange table green.
VERIFY: npx vitest run test/core/slot.test.ts
acceptance_criteria: Types compile; slotKey stable + collision-free across differing fields; classifyChange table green for NEW/REMOVED/FREED/FILLED/SAME (incl 4->2 FILLED, 0->2 FREED); Slot zod rejects malformed input. VERIFY: npx vitest run test/core/slot.test.ts
notes:
courseRef union now has FIVE variants (5th backend added pre-execution): add ClubhouseRef{backend:'clubhouse', host, courseId, externalId} alongside TeeOnRef/EzlinksRef/ChronogolfRef/LinklineRef. backendId union gains 'clubhouse'.
Generalize LinklineRef -> TeiUnifyRef{backend:'tei-unify', host, courseId} (host already present; now serves gateway.linklineonline.ca AND gateway.golfthe6ix.com). backendId 'linkline' -> 'tei-unify'. courseRef union still 5 variants (tee-on, ezlinks, chronogolf, tei-unify, clubhouse).

title: Core: polite rate limiter + Poller fan-out
description:
Enforces the legal/anti-ban policy (tee-times-xah) and drives the cache. See test-strategy Module 3. Depends on the AvailabilityAdapter INTERFACE only (inject adapters), NOT concrete adapters — testable with stubs.

WHAT:
- RateLimiter: <=4 req/course/hr, serial + jitter, exponential backoff on 429, HARD-STOP for the rest of the day on 403/captcha (no retry-through, no rotation, no challenge-solving).
- Poller.runCycle(adapters, store): for each (courseId,date) from listCoursesToPoll(), pick the adapter for that course's backend, call listSlots under the limiter, write result via putSnapshot. On AdapterError: DO NOT write [] (that would read as 'no times'); mark the course's backend health and leave prior snapshot intact (empty-vs-broken, I1). One adapter throwing never sinks the cycle.

FILES: src/poller/rate-limiter.ts, src/poller/poller.ts.
UNIT (test/poller/*.unit.test.ts): limiter blocks the 5th req within an hour; jitter+serial ordering; 429 backoff schedule; 403 -> hard-stop (subsequent calls for that backend suppressed same day); one-stub-throws -> cycle completes others.
INTEGRATION (test/poller/poller.integration.test.ts, REAL sqlite store + stub adapters mixed OK/throw/429/empty): a full cycle writes healthy snapshots for OK, leaves prior intact for throw, records blocked backend; blocked course stays suppressed next cycle.
ACCEPTANCE: limiter + empty-vs-broken write semantics proven against a real store.
VERIFY: npx vitest run test/poller
acceptance_criteria: Limiter blocks 5th req/course/hr; 429 backoff; 403->hard-stop suppresses that backend same day; one stub adapter throwing does not sink the cycle; empty([]) vs broken(AdapterError) write semantics correct against a real store; blocked course stays suppressed next cycle. VERIFY: npx vitest run test/poller

title: Core: AvailabilityStore (SQLite poll-cache + 2-deep snapshots + TTL/stale)
description:
The spine both search and watcher read from. See DESIGN (AvailabilityStore) + test-strategy Module 2 + G5/G6.

WHAT: AvailabilityStore interface + SQLite impl (better-sqlite3):
- putSnapshot(courseId, date, slots, fetchedAt): writes current, rotates previous; retains exactly 2-deep (current + prior) per (courseId,date) (G6).
- getSlots(courseId, date) -> {slots, fetchedAt, stale:bool} | MISS. stale = age > TTL. TTL default 15 min, config-driven (G5). MISS is distinct from an empty snapshot (empty = real 'no times').
- getSnapshotsForDiff(courseId,date) -> {prev?, curr?} for the watcher.
- listCoursesToPoll() -> {courseId,date}[] from the registry x the active date window.

FILES: src/store/store.ts (interface), src/store/sqlite-store.ts, src/store/schema.sql.
UNIT (test/store/store.unit.test.ts): TTL boundary just-under (fresh) vs just-over (stale-but-served); MISS != empty snapshot; classifyChange inputs come out of getSnapshotsForDiff correctly; per-(course,date) isolation.
INTEGRATION (test/store/store.integration.test.ts, REAL sqlite temp file, NOT mocked): put->read-back->advance clock past TTL->stale flag; putSnapshot twice->prior retained, third->oldest dropped (2-deep); reopen DB file->data persists; concurrent read during write returns a consistent snapshot.
ACCEPTANCE: real-sqlite integration green incl persistence-across-reopen + 2-deep retention.
VERIFY: npx vitest run test/store
acceptance_criteria: Real-sqlite integration green: put->read-back; TTL boundary just-under=fresh / just-over=stale-but-served; MISS != empty snapshot; 2-deep retention (third put drops oldest); data persists across DB reopen; consistent read during write. VERIFY: npx vitest run test/store

title: Adapter: Tee-On (the 50% coverage anchor; reference adapter)
description:
Highest-coverage (4/8) and easiest (anonymous HTML, no auth). Proves the seam; the reference all other adapters mirror. See tee-times-1ox + test-strategy Module 1.

WHAT: AvailabilityAdapter for backend 'tee-on'. Build the WebBookingSearchSteps / WebBookingAllTimesLanding request from TeeOnRef{courseCode,courseGroupId} + date + query; anonymous GET (honest UA, no auth/cookies); parse the returned HTML slot list with cheerio into Slot[]; convert times to course-local (I2); set bookingUrl to the course's own Tee-On booking page (I3). Classify failures: Cloudflare/blocked->AdapterError kind 'blocked'; markup-not-parseable->'parse'; network->'network'. Empty tee sheet -> [] (real 'no times'), NEVER conflated with blocked (I1).

UNIT (test/adapters/tee-on.unit.test.ts): golden-fixture parse -> exact expected Slot[] (assert time/date/holes/spotsAvailable/bookingUrl); empty-sheet fixture -> []; malformed fixture -> throws parse AdapterError; tz/DST correctness; deep-link shape.

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/adapters/tee-on

Tee-On flow confirmed as 3 servlet steps under /PubGolf/servlet/com.teeon.teesheet.servlets.golfersection.: (1) WebBookingAllTimesLanding?CourseCode={CODE}&CourseGroupID={GRP} = date grid; (2) WebBookingSearchSteps?CourseGroupID={GRP}&CourseCode={CODE}&Date=YYYY-MM-DD = GET search form; times dropdown SearchTime has 15-min-grid <option value='HH:MM'>H:MM am/pm</option>; (3) WebBookingSearchResults = POST availability with body Date=YYYY-MM-DD&SearchTime=(''=any)&Holes=(18|9)&Players=(1-4)&CourseId{CODE}={CODE}&CourseGroupID={GRP}&Referrer=. title: OPEN-QUESTION (user): browser devtools captures needed to finalize 3 adapters
description:
Research hit a hard wall that only a real browser session (ideally the user's, since he has logins/lockers) can resolve. Three of the four backends hide their availability endpoint behind an SPA XHR that non-browser web research (WebFetch/WebSearch) could not observe - all three returned Cloudflare 403/525 or an empty SPA shell. Each needs ONE devtools capture to convert 'unconfirmed' into an adapter spec.

ASK THE USER (or have a browser-capable follow-up spike do it): for each URL below, open it in a real browser, open DevTools -> Network -> filter to Fetch/XHR, load/search availability, and record: (a) the availability request URL (full path + query), (b) request headers/auth (any bearer token, cookie, api-key), (c) the response JSON schema (which fields carry time / holes / spots / price / booking link), (d) the numeric/UUID course identifiers.

1. Chronogolf - https://www.chronogolf.com/club/banty-s-roost-golf-club (pick a date). Confirm the anonymous marketplace teetimes endpoint + club_id/course_id/affiliation_type_id. (Sanctioned alt: Partner API v2, needs Lightspeed credentials.)
2. LinkLine - https://www.linklineonline.ca/web/tee-times?filters={"CourseID":["GA"]}. Confirm the JSON endpoint, whether the read path is anonymous or needs a session/bearer token, and the schema. Also confirm the CourseID code for each of the user's ClubLink courses.
3. EZLinks - https://lakeviewgc.ezlinksgolf.com and https://braeben.ezlinksgolf.com (#!/search). Confirm the gnsvc.com XHR path + facility/channel id + whether an honest bot can reach it or Cloudflare blocks non-browser clients (=> need headless/stealth or the GolfNow Affiliate/BDS partner API).

Tee-On (tee-times-1ox) is anonymous HTML and needs only a lightweight confirm (GET vs POST of the search submit + the slot-row HTML structure) - not blocked on the user.

ALSO for the user: confirm the CourseCode + CourseGroupID for his Tee-On courses (CENT is known/home + member PUB1695967; LOGC grp 10880, MTNE/Mount Nemo grp 11761, CROS grp 11115 provided - just verify CourseCodes) and provide his EZLinks/Chronogolf/LinkLine course identifiers if he has them handy. Relates to tee-times-1k3 (course-list open question).

title: RESEARCH: Architecture implications - MULTI-ADAPTER REQUIRED at launch + common interface shape
description:
SYNTHESIS of the per-backend findings (Tee-On tee-times-1ox, EZLinks tee-times-v6c, Chronogolf tee-times-f5f, LinkLine tee-times-853, prior-art tee-times-iyi, legal tee-times-xah).

DECISION: BUILD A MULTI-ADAPTER SEAM FROM DAY ONE. The single-Tee-On-adapter option is DEAD on the numbers. The user's 8 courses span 4 backends: Tee-On 4 (50%), EZLinks 2 (25%), Chronogolf 1 (12.5%), LinkLine 1 (12.5%). Tee-On alone = 50%, which FAILS the charter's 'strong majority' success bar (partial coverage = failure). To clear a strong majority you must ship at least Tee-On + EZLinks (75%) or Tee-On + EZLinks + one more. So the multi-adapter architecture is not a future nicety - it is required to meet the launch success criterion.

STRUCTURAL SIMILARITY (informs the interface): all 4 backends share the SAME conceptual query - (course identifier) + date + players/holes -> list of slots - and all 4 ultimately yield structured availability (Tee-On as parseable HTML; EZLinks/Chronogolf/LinkLine as JSON behind an SPA). So a common OUTPUT interface fits ALL 4 cleanly. What DIVERGES is the fetch mechanics + tenant addressing, not the data model. => Design: ONE normalized model + ONE common adapter interface + N backend-specific fetchers.

COMMON ADAPTER INTERFACE (proposed):
  interface AvailabilityAdapter {
    listSlots(courseRef, date, {players, holes}) -> Slot[]
  }
  type Slot = { courseId, date, time (local), holes: 9|18, spotsAvailable, price?, bookingUrl, raw? }
  courseRef is backend-specific config: Tee-On {CourseCode, CourseGroupID}; EZLinks {subdomain, facilityId/channelId}; Chronogolf {clubId, courseId, affiliationTypeId}; LinkLine {host, CourseID}. bookingUrl always deep-links back to the course's own booking page (metasearch, book-direct - never intermediary).
Aggregator fans out across adapters, normalizes to Slot[], merges into one time-sorted view. Adapters are independently testable with golden fixtures; add-a-course = add config (or a new adapter for a new backend).

FETCH-MECHANICS TIERS (per-adapter effort, NOT uniform):
  - Tee-On (EASIEST, do FIRST): anonymous, plain servlet, HTML parse. No auth, least defended. Highest coverage (50%). Reference adapter.
  - Chronogolf (MIDDLE): clean JSON once one devtools capture yields the endpoint + club/course ids; Cloudflare-fronted so honest-UA + low-rate. Generalizes to future Chronogolf courses.
  - LinkLine (MODERATE, gated on 1 capture): clean CourseID tenant model, but endpoint+read-auth+schema all invisible until a live browser/devtools capture; likely needs SPA token-bootstrap replay or headless.
  - EZLinks (HARDEST, highest risk): = GolfNow/NBC backend, Cloudflare-defended, endpoint hidden, sanctioned route is a gated OAuth partner API. Likely needs headless/stealth browser OR partner-API application OR accept as deep-link-only degraded tier.

TOP 3 TECHNICAL RISKS TO THE AGGREGATION WEDGE:
  1. EZLinks/GolfNow anti-bot (Cloudflare + OAuth-gated partner API) blocks the 2 courses (25%) needed to clear a strong majority. If EZLinks proves infeasible cheaply, coverage stalls at Tee-On+Chronogolf+LinkLine = 75% (6/8) - still a strong majority, so EZLinks is important but not single-point-fatal. MITIGATION: sequence EZLinks last; have a deep-link-only fallback tier so an un-scrapable backend still appears in results (link out) rather than being absent.
  2. SPA/devtools dependency for 3 of 4 backends: EZLinks, Chronogolf, LinkLine all hide the real availability endpoint behind an SPA XHR that non-browser research could not capture. Each needs ONE real-browser devtools capture (endpoint URL + auth + JSON schema) before its adapter can be written. This is a concrete, cheap, upfront planning task - but it is a hard prerequisite. Create a spike per backend.
  3. Brittleness of scraped contracts: none of these is a stable public API (Tee-On HTML markup; reverse-engineered JSON paths that can change). MITIGATION: golden-fixture parser tests per adapter, health-check/alerting when an adapter returns 0 slots or fails to parse, and the honest-UA/polite-rate policy (bead tee-times-xah) to avoid IP bans that masquerade as outages.

REUSE POSTURE: build fresh (Layer-3); no reusable aggregator library exists (tee-times-iyi). Reference tee-sniper/lets-go-golfing for the watcher fast-follow, TeeTimeFinder for aggregator UX.

RECOMMENDED BUILD SEQUENCE: (1) common interface + normalized Slot model + aggregator skeleton; (2) Tee-On adapter (50%, easiest, proves the seam); (3) devtools-capture spikes for Chronogolf + LinkLine, then their adapters (->75%); (4) EZLinks adapter or partner-API or deep-link fallback (->100% or graceful-degraded); (5) watcher fast-follow on top of the poll cache.
notes: [no-test] RESEARCH/open-question artifact (no code). Findings consumed by ARCHITECTURE + DECOMPOSITION. azn superseded by capture spikes tee-times-r4w (Chronogolf), tee-times-4vw (LinkLine), tee-times-tt6 (EZLinks).

title: RESEARCH: LinkLine Online (TEI Unify) availability access pattern (1 course)
description:
BACKEND: LinkLine Online (linklineonline.ca). Covers 1 of the user's 8 courses (12.5%): linklineonline.ca/web/tee-times?filters={"CourseID":["GA"]}. SPA.

WHAT IT ACTUALLY IS: linklineonline.ca is ClubLink's branded deployment of the 'Unify Platform' by Total e Integrated (TEI), a Toronto golf/hospitality software vendor (older brand: IBS / goibsvision.com). Every /web/tee-times page renders the same shell <title>Total e Integrated | Unify Platform</title>. ASP.NET/DotNetNuke backend under the SPA. ClubLink is Canada's largest operator (~50 courses) so this backend generalizes well if the user adds more ClubLink/TEI courses.

ACCESS VERDICT: client-side SPA -> availability via separate XHR/JSON call. The filters={JSON} URL param is parsed CLIENT-SIDE by the SPA router (fetching /web/tee-times returns only the empty app shell, no slots, even with the filters param). The backing JSON endpoint is UNCONFIRMED - needs browser devtools. Guessed paths (/api/tee-times etc.) fell through to the SPA catch-all (returned shell, not JSON), confirming the real API base is non-obvious and undocumented. No public API docs, NO GitHub prior art for LinkLine/TEI/goibsvision (you would be first).

AUTH: MIXED / partly unconfirmed. Booking definitely requires login (member number + password). Whether READING availability is anonymous is unconfirmed - ClubLink members' Players Club is login-gated, but ClubLink also runs public daily-fee booking on the same Unify SPA (clublink.ca/daily-fee-golf/book/), and the CourseID codes look like daily-fee courses. Read path may be anonymous or may need an anonymous bearer/session token minted by the SPA on load. FLAG: read-auth = unconfirmed; booking-auth = required.

ANTI-BOT: Cloudflare/WAF present. www.linklineonline.ca returned HTTP 525 (Cloudflare origin TLS error) to non-browser fetch; old ww5.goibsvision.com/.../Browse returned 403. No honorable robots.txt served (apex serves SPA shell for /robots.txt; www 525). Expect JS challenge / TLS-fingerprint / header scrutiny on any plain HTTP client.

TENANT IDENTITY (CONFIRMED clean + multi-tenant-friendly): ONE shared host per region, many courses keyed by a CourseID code ARRAY. Real examples: linklineonline.ca ...filters={"CourseID":["ANY","DC1","DC2","DC3","DC"]} (Canada; note special 'ANY'); us.linklineonline.ca ...["PAC","PAP","PAO"] (US host); user's course = ["GA"]. So one LinkLine adapter parameterized by CourseID covers all ClubLink courses; Canadian courses on linklineonline.ca, US on us.linklineonline.ca. Non-ClubLink TEI customers live on *.totaleintegrated.net subdomains (one per operator).

RESPONSE SHAPE: UNCONFIRMED (never saw real JSON - SPA, no devtools). Per TEI Unify feature set, per-slot object likely carries tee datetime, CourseID, holes (9/18), open player count, rate/price (possibly by customer type), cart option, reservation id. Treat as hypothesized until captured.

DIFFICULTY VERDICT: MODERATE, gated on ONE unknown. URL/tenant model is clean (one host, CourseID array). But EVERYTHING past the SPA shell (endpoint, read-auth, JSON schema) is invisible to non-browser tooling and cannot be resolved without a live devtools/headless capture. Until that capture, the adapter cannot be written. TOP RISK: Cloudflare (525/403) + likely token/session auth on the read path => adapter may need to replay the SPA token-bootstrap or run headless, not a bare JSON GET. Secondary: CourseID codes are opaque/undocumented, enumerate per course by hand.

IMMEDIATE NEXT STEP (outside read-only research): load linklineonline.ca/web/tee-times?filters={"CourseID":["GA"]} in a real browser, Network tab filtered to XHR/Fetch, record the availability request URL + headers/auth + response JSON. That single capture converts every 'unconfirmed' above into a spec.

EVIDENCE URLs fetched: www.linklineonline.ca/ (525), .../web/tee-times?filters={CourseID:[GA]} (525), linklineonline.ca/web/tee-times (SPA shell), us.linklineonline.ca/web/tee-times?filters=[RE] (SPA shell), bidwellpark.totaleintegrated.net/web/tee-times (SPA shell), ww5.goibsvision.com/WebRes/Club/LinkLineOnLine/Browse (403); vendor totaleintegrated.com.

title: RESEARCH: Chronogolf/Lightspeed availability access pattern (1 course, Banty's Roost)
description:
BACKEND: Chronogolf / Lightspeed Golf (chronogolf.com). Covers 1 of the user's 8 courses (12.5%): chronogolf.com/club/banty-s-roost-golf-club?date=... SPA, JSON backend.

ACCESS VERDICT: JSON API, richest/cleanest data model of the 4 backends, but two access surfaces:
 (1) SANCTIONED Partner API v2 (partner-api.docs.chronogolf.com): REST + JSON:API. Tee times endpoint CONFIRMED shape:
     GET https://<host>/partner_api/v2/organizations/{ORG_UUID}/teetimes?filter[date]=YYYY-MM-DD&filter[course]={COURSE_UUID}&custom_params[player_types][]={PLAYER_TYPE_UUID}&custom_params[with_pricing]=true
     Uses UUIDs. Rate limit ~200 req/min. Requires partner/OAuth credentials provisioned by Lightspeed (golf.api@lightspeedhq.com) — a business gate, not a scraping gate.
 (2) ANONYMOUS consumer/marketplace JSON API (what the public chronogolf.com booking widget calls with no login). This is the likely low-effort path for a personal tool. Structure keyed by club_id/course_id, date (YYYY-MM-DD), nb_holes, affiliation_type_id (player type: Public/Member). EXACT public path UNCONFIRMED this session: www.chronogolf.com/club/banty-s-roost-golf-club and /marketplace/... returned HTTP 403 to non-browser fetch (Cloudflare), and robots.txt disallows /private_api/ and /reservations/ (the paths the widget hits). The commonly-referenced reverse-engineered public endpoint pattern is /marketplace/v2/teetimes?date=...&course_ids[]=...&nb_holes=18&affiliation_type_ids[]=... but this MUST be confirmed with a real-browser devtools capture on the Banty's Roost page (do NOT code against the guessed path).

AUTH: reading availability is anonymous on the consumer surface (no login to browse); login only to complete a booking. Partner API is credential-gated.

ANTI-BOT: moderate. Cloudflare fronts the consumer site (403 to non-browser). Not as hostile as EZLinks/GolfNow but expect header/UA scrutiny on /private_api/.

TENANT IDENTITY: hierarchical Organization -> Club -> Course -> Teetime. Single shared host (chronogolf.com/.ca), NOT per-course subdomain. Club identified by club_id (UUID in v2), course by course_id, pricing by affiliation_type_id/player_type. Get Banty's Roost club_id/course_id from the page bootstrap JSON via devtools.

RESPONSE SHAPE: JSON tee time objects with start time, holes, and an array of green fees / player types each with price + restrictions. Best-structured of the 4 (price + spots inline).

DIFFICULTY VERDICT: MIDDLE. Clean JSON once the endpoint + club/course ids are captured. One-time devtools capture on the Banty's Roost page resolves everything. Single Chronogolf adapter would generalize to future Chronogolf courses (many exist).

TOP RISK: the anonymous marketplace endpoint sits behind Cloudflare + is robots-disallowed (/private_api/); a naive HTTP client may get 403. Mitigate with honest UA + low rate, or use the sanctioned Partner API if the user wants durability for more Chronogolf courses later.

OPEN CONFIRM (cheap): browser devtools on chronogolf.com/club/banty-s-roost-golf-club -> record the availability XHR URL, the club_id/course_id/affiliation_type_id, and the JSON schema.

EVIDENCE URLs: www.chronogolf.com/robots.txt (Disallow /private_api/, /reservations/), partner-api.docs.chronogolf.com (v2 teetimes params confirmed), www.chronogolf.com/club/banty-s-roost-golf-club (403 Cloudflare).

title: RESEARCH: EZLinks availability access pattern (2 courses; GolfNow/NBC backend, hardest)
description:
BACKEND: EZLinks (ezlinksgolf.com). Covers 2 of the user's 8 courses (25%): lakeviewgc.ezlinksgolf.com and braeben.ezlinksgolf.com. Per-course vanity SUBDOMAIN, SPA with #!/search route.

CRITICAL FRAMING: EZLinks IS GolfNow / NBC Sports Next. www.ezlinksgolf.com 301-redirects to business.golfnow.com. The availability backend is the GolfNow distribution platform (gnsvc.com; object model channel -> facility -> tee time). This is the SAME infrastructure as GolfNow.com and is the MOST bot-hostile of the user's 4 backends.

ACCESS VERDICT: JSON API behind an SPA, but actively defended. Reading slots is anonymous/guest (no user login to browse), BUT:
 - Cloudflare fronts the whole estate. Every live host (both course subdomains, gnsvc.com API hosts, api-affiliates.gnsvc.com docs) returned HTTP 403 to non-browser fetch while robots.txt served fine => managed bot rule blocking headless/flagged UAs.
 - robots.txt (verbatim, lakeviewgc + braeben identical) is Cloudflare's Content-Signals template: default Allow: / with Content-Signal search=yes,ai-train=no,use=reference; explicit Disallow: / for Amazonbot, ClaudeBot, GPTBot, CCBot, Google-Extended, Bytespider, meta-externalagent, CloudflareBrowserRenderingCrawler, etc. www.ezlinksgolf.com/robots.txt -> 301 business.golfnow.com.
 - The concrete consumer XHR path used by <subdomain>/#!/search is UNCONFIRMED (behind SPA, blocked by Cloudflare from non-browser tooling). Near-certainly a gnsvc.com REST/JSON call keyed by a numeric facility/channel id. NOT resolvable without a real-browser devtools capture.

TENANT IDENTITY: the subdomain (lakeviewgc, braeben) is a vanity front end; the API request carries a NUMERIC facility id (+ channel id), not the subdomain string. Legacy EZTEEPRO server API used SponsorID + CourseID (ints). SPA maps subdomain->numeric id at bootstrap.

SANCTIONED PATH: GolfNow Affiliate / Bulk Data Service (BDS) partner API — OAuth2, partner-approved, gated (affiliate.gnsvc.com, api-affiliates.gnsvc.com, v2.bds.gnsvc.com). This is the durable/legal read route but requires applying to the partner program.

RESPONSE SHAPE (from legacy EZTEEPRO spec 2.20): per slot has TeeTimeDate, CourseID, holes, FeeString (FeeID/FeeDescription/FeeAmount/IncludeCart), ReserveString (opaque booking token). Modern GolfNow JSON field names unconfirmed (OAuth-gated docs).

DIFFICULTY VERDICT: HARDEST of the 4. Not a plain JSON GET. A read-only adapter needs (1) a real/stealth browser context or captured session token to pass Cloudflare, (2) devtools capture of the gnsvc.com XHR path, (3) facility-id resolution per subdomain. TOP RISK: same GolfNow/NBC backend as GolfNow.com — Cloudflare-defended + explicit anti-bot robots + paid partner API is the intended route => an unofficial scraper is both technically brittle (challenge/IP-block) and squarely against ToS. See legal finding: EZLinks/GolfNow is the one backend to be genuinely conservative with.

RECOMMENDATION for planning: budget these 2 courses as high-effort/high-risk. Options in priority order: (a) apply for GolfNow Affiliate/BDS partner API; (b) headless-browser (Playwright stealth) adapter capturing the XHR; (c) accept these 2 as a degraded/deep-link-only tier (link out to the course booking page without live aggregation) if (a)/(b) prove too costly.

EVIDENCE URLs fetched: lakeviewgc.ezlinksgolf.com/robots.txt, braeben.ezlinksgolf.com/robots.txt, www.ezlinksgolf.com/robots.txt (->business.golfnow.com), EZTEEPRO API Spec 2.20 (pdfcoffee mirror), v2.bds.gnsvc.com/help, affiliate.gnsvc.com/getting-started, api-affiliates.gnsvc.com/documentation/#!/search (all live hosts 403).

title: RESEARCH: Tee-On availability endpoint contract (primary backend, 4 of 8 courses)
description:
BACKEND: Tee-On (tee-on.com). Covers 4 of the user's 8 courses (50%): CENT (user home, member PUB1695967), LOGC (CourseGroupID 10880), MTNE/Mount Nemo (grp 11761), CROS (grp 11115).

ACCESS VERDICT: Anonymous HTML, no JSON. Availability is readable WITHOUT login. Confirmed by fetching a live course search page (CourseCode=OEGC, CourseGroupID=10621): the page renders a search form (date / time / holes 18|9 / players 4|3|2|1) with an auto-issued anonymous locker (hidden input LockerString=OEGC<epoch> (Public Golfer)1|0). No sign-in wall. Results are a server-rendered HTML tee-time list that must be PARSED (no JSON API).

ENDPOINT: single shared host + servlet path for ALL courses (NOT per-course subdomains/tenants):
  https://www.tee-on.com/PubGolf/servlet/com.teeon.teesheet.servlets.golfersection.WebBookingSearchSteps
Course is identified by two params: CourseCode (e.g. CENT, OEGC, LOGC, MTNE, CROS) + CourseGroupID (numeric). Entry/landing variant seen: ?FromTrailSearch=true&FromCourseWebsite=true&CourseCode=OEGC&CourseGroupID=10621.

DECODED SEARCH REQUEST (from the user's working URL):
  WebBookingSearchSteps?UnlockTime0=CENT|W|2026-06-15|16:20|E|18:20|99 &UnlockTime1=CENT|W|2026-06-15|17:20|E|19:20|99 &LockerString=Dylan%20Delli%20Colli%20(PUB1695967)1|0 &Referrer=www.tee-on.com
Param decode (pipe-delimited UnlockTimeN, one per desired time-window, repeatable):
  field0=CourseCode (CENT); field1=W (search/booking-type flag); field2=date YYYY-MM-DD; field3=window START HH:MM; field4=E (start/end separator marker); field5=window END HH:MM; field6=99 (catch-all flag, holes/players = any).
  LockerString = golfer display name + (memberID) + trailing '1|0'. For anonymous availability the site auto-issues a 'Public Golfer' locker; the member locker only personalizes/enables actual booking. => availability does NOT require the member id.
  Referrer=www.tee-on.com.

SINGLE-ADAPTER-ACROSS-4-COURSES: one Tee-On adapter serves all 4 user courses by varying CourseCode + CourseGroupID; no per-course auth.

POLLING: GET WebBookingSearchSteps with UnlockTimeN window(s) + a Public Golfer LockerString; parse the returned HTML slot list into {date,time,holes,spots,bookingUrl}. bookingUrl = the same servlet URL pre-filled for that course/date (deep-link back to Tee-On, satisfying book-direct). Politely: robots.txt Disallow: / (see legal finding) => low rate, cache, honest UA. See legal finding for rate policy.

OPEN CONFIRM (cheap, one browser session): (1) exact GET vs POST of the submitted search (form uses JS document.getElementById('form').submit(); method not visible in fetched markup) and (2) the exact HTML structure of the returned slot rows (for the parser). Both need one real-browser capture against a live CourseCode.

RISK: HTML parser is brittle to markup changes (Tee-On is a legacy Java servlet app, low churn but no contract). Mitigate with a golden-HTML fixture + parser test.

EVIDENCE URLs fetched: tee-on.com/robots.txt; WebBookingSearchSteps?...CourseCode=OEGC&CourseGroupID=10621 (live form); WebSearch confirmed CourseCode/CourseGroupID pattern across OEGC/SIMO/HNTD courses + single-username-works-network-wide.

title: Fast-follow: Watcher / Alert engine (snapshot diff -> rule match -> sink)
description:
The scarcity-hunt fast-follow. Reads snapshots from the store, never re-polls. See test-strategy Module 5 + G1/G2. Group-wide rules, NO per-user model (A2).

WHAT:
- AlertRule {courseIds[], dayOrDateWindow, timeWindow, minPlayers?, minSpots?, holes?} (group-wide).
- runWatch(store, rules, sink): for each watched (course,date), diff prev vs curr via classifyChange (slot-model); emit an alert for transitions NEW or FREED (G1: a freed spot = a cancellation) that match a rule. Idempotency: never emit the same slotKey-transition twice across cycles (track emitted keys). Broken-curr guard: if curr snapshot is missing/errored, DO NOT interpret as mass-cancellation (no REMOVED storm). Cold-start (no prev): seed baseline, do NOT alert on the entire sheet.
- NotificationSink interface {send(alert)} — abstract; concrete channel is bead notify-channel.

FILES: src/watcher/watcher.ts, src/watcher/rules.ts, src/watcher/sink.ts (interface only).
UNIT (test/watcher/watcher.unit.test.ts): NEW + FREED detection; rule matching; idempotency (no dup across 2 cycles); appear-then-disappear ghost within an interval; broken-curr -> no storm; cold-start -> no storm.
INTEGRATION (test/watcher/watcher.integration.test.ts, REAL store 2 snapshots + stub sink): snapshot->diff->sink.send called exactly for matching NEW/FREED; multi-cycle dedupe holds.
ACCEPTANCE: idempotency + broken-curr guard green against a real store.
VERIFY: npx vitest run test/watcher
acceptance_criteria: NEW + FREED detection vs prior snapshot; group-wide rule matching; idempotency (no dup alert across 2 cycles); appear-then-disappear ghost handled; broken-curr -> no mass-cancel storm; cold-start -> no storm; INTEGRATION against real store 2 snapshots + stub sink. VERIFY: npx vitest run test/watcher

title: Core: coverage-% acceptance + backend health rollup
description:
Makes the charter success bar executable: partial coverage = failure. See test-strategy Module 7.

title: RESEARCH: Legal/ToS/anti-bot posture + recommended polling policy (private 5-user tool)
description:
SCOPE: risk-framing for a PRIVATE, non-commercial tool for the user + ~5-person foursome. NOT reselling, NOT a public service, NOT taking payment/holding inventory - links out to each course's own booking page. Not legal advice.

LEGAL BOTTOM LINE: negligible legal risk. Scraping public, logged-out, non-authenticated availability for personal small-group use sits squarely in the protected zone of US scraping case law: hiQ v. LinkedIn (9th Cir 2022 - scraping public data likely not 'without authorization' under CFAA), Van Buren v. US (SCOTUS 2021 - narrows CFAA), Meta v. Bright Data (N.D.Cal 2024 - FB terms don't bar logged-OFF scraping), X Corp v. Bright Data (2024 - trespass-to-chattels needs server damage ordinary scraping doesn't cause). hiQ's 00k loss rested on breach-of-contract + using FAKE ACCOUNTS to reach password-protected pages. THE BRIGHT LINE: never log in, never use member/course credentials, never create fake accounts, never defeat a captcha or bypass an explicit IP block. Auth-walled scraping generates real liability; public-page polling does not.

robots.txt POSTURE (all 4 backends disallow crawling the availability endpoints; verbatim):
 - tee-on.com: 'User-agent: * / Disallow: / / Allow: /index.html' (tee sheets blocked).
 - chronogolf.com: not a blanket block, but 'Disallow: /private_api/' and '/reservations/' - the exact paths availability uses.
 - EZLinks (lakeviewgc/braeben.ezlinksgolf.com): Cloudflare Content-Signals template, Allow: / default but Disallow: / for named AI/bot UAs (ClaudeBot, GPTBot, CCBot...).
 - LinkLine (linklineonline.ca): no honorable robots served (Cloudflare 525 on www; apex serves SPA shell for /robots.txt).
robots.txt is convention (not law, not contract) but is the clearest published owner-intent signal - treat as etiquette that keeps you un-blocked.

ToS RED LINES (treat as hard don'ts): (1) circumventing auth/login or fake accounts; (2) reselling/redistributing data; (3) degrading service performance; (4) circumventing rate limits/captchas/IP blocks; (5) bulk harvesting at scale. The user's tool does none of these. GolfNow's own terms page 403'd to automated fetch - GolfNow/NBC (which OWNS EZLinks) is the most litigious/aggressive actor and the one backend to be genuinely conservative with (prefer deep-linking over scraping where possible).

ANTI-BOT REALITY: Cloudflare/WAF on EZLinks(GolfNow), Chronogolf, LinkLine (403/525 to non-browser). Tee-On is a plain legacy servlet (least defended). Real risk is TECHNICAL (429->IP ban, captcha), not legal.

RECOMMENDED POLLING POLICY (conservative, respectful - apply to ALL adapters):
 - Rate: 1 req/course every 15-30 min during golf hours (~6am-8pm local); <=4 req/course/hr; well under 100/day/course.
 - Cache: 10-15 min server-side TTL per course; serve all 5 users from cache; NEVER let a user action trigger a live upstream hit (decouple UI reads from the poller).
 - Concurrency: serial, one course at a time, 2-5s jitter; never burst all courses at once.
 - Off-peak: minimal/no polling overnight.
 - User-Agent: HONEST identifying UA with contact (e.g. 'FoursomeTeeTimes/1.0 (personal; contact you@email)'). Do NOT spoof a browser to evade detection (bad-faith signal). NOTE tension: EZLinks/Cloudflare may 403 an honest bot UA - if so, that backend is telling you no; prefer the sanctioned partner API or a deep-link-only tier rather than UA-spoofing to punch through.
 - Backoff: on 429 exponential from 5 min -> 1-2 hr cap; on 403/captcha/hard-block STOP that course for the day and alert self, do NOT retry-through / rotate IPs / solve challenge.
 - Conditional requests: honor ETag / If-Modified-Since where offered.
 - Kill switch: per-course disable flag; honor any request to stop.

EVIDENCE URLs fetched: tee-on.com/robots.txt, foreupsoftware.com/robots.txt, www.chronogolf.com/robots.txt, lakeviewgc/braeben.ezlinksgolf.com/robots.txt, gem/cityofcalgarygolf.cps.golf/robots.txt, golfnow.com/support/about-us/terms (403), archive.foreupgolf.com/terms-of-service; case-law sources EFF/Morgan Lewis/Proskauer/Skadden on hiQ, Van Buren, Meta & X v. Bright Data.

title: RESEARCH: Prior art / reuse for tee-time aggregation (verdict: build fresh, reference-only)
description:
QUESTION: reuse an existing open-source tee-time aggregator library (Layer-1), or build fresh referencing prior art (Layer-3)?

BOTTOM LINE: BUILD FRESH (Layer-3). There is NO maintained, licensed, multi-backend tee-time aggregation LIBRARY. Every project found is a single-course personal booking bot or single-backend monitor. Reusable value = endpoint knowledge + architecture patterns, NOT code. Critically, NONE of the user's 4 actual backends (Tee-On, EZLinks, Chronogolf, LinkLine) has usable open-source prior art documenting its availability contract - Tee-On and LinkLine have ZERO prior art (user would be first); EZLinks/Chronogolf only have partner-API docs, not scrapers.

MOST RELEVANT PROJECTS (best first):
1. ehickox/teetime_booker (github.com/ehickox/teetime_booker) - Python/requests, MIT. Reference-only but the single most valuable find: documents the ForeUp JSON contract (GET /index.php/api/booking/times?...schedule_id&api_key=no_limits; POST /index.php/api/booking/users/login). NOTE: ForeUp is NOT in the user's course set - value is generality for future courses only.
2. stebennett/tee-sniper (github.com/stebennett/tee-sniper) - Python/FastAPI+Redis+React, MIT, ACTIVELY maintained (v4.1.0 May 2026). Targets a generic BRS-style member-login HTML club (not user's backends). BEST ARCHITECTURE REFERENCE for the watcher/notify fast-follow: persisted 'wanted' requests + daily poll worker + Twilio SMS + an MCP server. Reference-only (its client is for another backend).
3. Ay1tsMe/TeeTimeFinder (github.com/Ay1tsMe/TeeTimeFinder) - Go, MIT, recent (Dec 2025). Only project with genuine multi-course AGGREGATION intent (search N courses at once). Targets MiClub/Sagacity (AU, not user's backends). Best reference for the aggregator UX (config of N courses -> unified search).
4. smaldd14/lets-go-golfing (github.com/smaldd14/lets-go-golfing) - Java/Spring/Temporal, GolfNow via Browserbase headless. Reference for the poll->diff->alert-on-NEW monitoring pattern with durable workflow. No endpoint to lift (drives a browser).
5. mrtrosen/foreup-autores - ForeUp via Playwright DOM automation, NO license -> not reusable.
6. pabrodez/golf-now-alerter (archived 2022, no license), joshkarbi/golf-tee-time-bot (obfuscated) - not useful.
Also: niallhodgen/tee-time-booker (BRS API, reference if BRS ever added); aleplusplus/golfnow-node-js (GolfNow). Chronogolf: only official partner API (partner-api.docs.chronogolf.com), no OSS scraper. Tee-On / LinkLine / Club Prophet: NO OSS scraper found.

LAYER CALLS PER COMPONENT:
 - Availability adapters (all 4 backends): Layer-3 BUILD clean-room. No reusable library; per-backend endpoints undocumented in OSS. Reference ehickox for JSON-contract shape discipline.
 - Aggregation/normalization core: Layer-3 BUILD. Model UX on TeeTimeFinder.
 - Watcher fast-follow: Layer-3 BUILD, but heavily reference tee-sniper (wanted-requests+worker+SMS/MCP) and lets-go-golfing (poll->diff->notify-new). Do not depend.
 - Licensing caveat: several relevant repos have NO license => cannot legally copy code; treat all as read-for-understanding only.

EVIDENCE URLs fetched: the github.com repos above (+ raw README/source for ehickox/teetime_booker, stebennett/tee-sniper booking_client.py), smaldore.dev/blog/temporal/tee-time-monitoring, foreup.docs.apiary.io, partner-api.docs.chronogolf.com.

title: Personal tee-time metasearch and slot watcher (Tee-On, book-direct)
description: Discovery intention; see charter (slug: personal-tee-time-metasearch-and-slot-watcher-tee-on-book-direct). Small-group (me + regular foursome, ~5 known people) fee-free tee-time tool: unified availability SEARCH across our 10+ favorite courses (the confirmed critical wedge) plus a fast-follow watcher for scarce/cancelled good slots. Metasearch that routes to each course's own direct booking page, NOT a GolfNow-style intermediary. Edge over GolfNow = coverage of the long tail (courses GolfNow doesn't even list), not just fee avoidance. KEY OPEN RISK: aggregation is the critical wedge and the 10+ courses may span multiple booking backends (Tee-On/ForeUp/Lightspeed-Chronogolf) — first RESEARCH task is mapping each course to its backend, which decides one-adapter vs multi-adapter at launch.
design:
# Architecture review (Gaudi --epic tee-times-z4v, 2026-07-02, greenfield / no source)

Adapted to SABLE ARCHITECTURE substage: the epic is intentionally childless (pre-DECOMPOSITION), so this is not a per-child review. It LOCKS the interface contract, tradeoff positions, module decomposition, and smell-avoidance invariants that DECOMPOSITION will split into implementation beads. Grounded in research beads tee-times-8uc (arch synthesis), 1ox/v6c/f5f/853 (per-backend), xah (legal/rate), iyi (prior art).

## Plain-language summary (read first)
This app is a "Kayak for tee times": it checks several golf-course booking websites, shows all the open times in one list, and sends you to the course's own page to book (no fees, we never take payment). The key structural idea is an **adapter** — a small piece of code, one per booking website, that knows how to talk to THAT site and hand back times in a single common format. The rest of the app never learns the messy per-site details; it only ever sees clean, uniform "slots." Because your 8 courses use 4 different booking systems, we need 4 adapters, all behind one shared shape. We also don't hammer these sites live on every search — we poll them politely in the background, remember the results for ~10-15 minutes (a **cache**), and serve searches and alerts from that memory. That one decision keeps the app fast, keeps us from getting IP-banned, and gives the slot-watcher a "before vs after" to compare so it can tell you when a new time opens up.

---

## Vocabulary (defined on first use, per Gaudi pedagogy)
- **Module** — a unit of code that owns one concept and hides its internals. Good modules are "deep": a simple interface hiding substantial work.
- **Interface / contract** — the small set of things a caller must know to use a module (the function signature + invariants + error modes). Everything else is hidden.
- **Adapter** — a module that translates a foreign/external system into our internal shape. Here: one per booking backend.
- **Seam** — the line where our code meets something that varies (here, "which booking backend"). A seam is *real* when 2+ implementations sit behind it, *hypothetical* when only one does. We have 4 → the seam is real and earns its keep.
- **Push vs pull** — does data get pushed to us (we're notified), or do we pull it (we go ask)? 
- **Polling vs WebSocket** — pull on a timer (polling) vs a live open connection that streams changes (WebSocket).
- **Cache strategy** — keeping a short-lived local copy of fetched data so we don't refetch on every read.

---

## Interface contracts locked

### 1. `AvailabilityAdapter` (the seam — one per backend)
```
interface AvailabilityAdapter {
  backendId: 'tee-on' | 'ezlinks' | 'chronogolf' | 'linkline'
  listSlots(courseRef, date, query: {players?, holes?}) -> Promise<Slot[]>
}
type Slot = {
  courseId,            // our stable internal id for the course
  backendId,           // provenance
  date,                // YYYY-MM-DD, course-local
  time,                // HH:MM, course-local (adapter owns tz conversion)
  holes,               // 9 | 18
  spotsAvailable,      // int
  price?,              // if the backend exposes it
  bookingUrl,          // ALWAYS a deep link to the course's OWN booking page
  raw?                 // opaque original payload, for debugging/tests only
}
type courseRef =       // backend-specific config, NOT positional args:
  | {backend:'tee-on',     courseCode, courseGroupId}
  | {backend:'ezlinks',    subdomain, facilityId /*+channelId*/}
  | {backend:'chronogolf', clubId, courseId, affiliationTypeId}
  | {backend:'linkline',   host, courseId}
```
**Invariants (locked):**
- (I1) An adapter returns normalized `Slot[]` or throws a typed `AdapterError` (`{backendId, courseId, kind: 'blocked'|'parse'|'network'|'auth', retryable}`). It NEVER returns backend-shaped data and NEVER returns `[]` to mean "I broke" — empty is a real, distinct "genuinely no times" signal (see Risk R3).
- (I2) ALL backend-specific logic (auth, HTML parsing, JSON path extraction, timezone, pagination) lives INSIDE the adapter. The aggregator only ever sees `Slot`. (Prevents the smells below.)
- (I3) `bookingUrl` always deep-links to the course's own booking page. We are metasearch, never intermediary — no payment, no inventory, no login.

### 2. `AvailabilityStore` (the shared poll cache — the spine)
```
getSlots(courseId, date) -> {slots: Slot[], fetchedAt, stale: bool} | MISS
putSnapshot(courseId, date, slots, fetchedAt)   // also retains prior snapshot for diffing
listCoursesToPoll() -> {courseId, date}[]        // drives the poller
```
Both the search path and the watcher read from here. The poller is the only writer.

---

## Tradeoffs locked (with positions + reasons)

| # | Tradeoff | Position | Why (forced or chosen) |
|---|---|---|---|
| #2 | Push vs pull | **PULL** | FORCED — no backend pushes availability. We poll. |
| #4 | Sync vs async | **ASYNC background poll; search served from cache** | CHOSEN — live-fetch-on-search would blow the polite-rate budget (xah: ≤4 req/course/hr), be slow (4 backends, Cloudflare), and expose Cloudflare failures to the user mid-search. Background poller keeps the cache warm; search reads cache. |
| #5 | Batch vs stream | **BATCH (scheduled poll cycles)** | FORCED by pull model. |
| #7 | Polling vs WebSocket | **POLLING** | FORCED — backends offer no live feed. **Consequence the user must know:** watcher freshness is bounded by poll interval (~10-15 min), NOT instant. Cancellation-sniping is "within ~15 min," not "the second it frees." |
| #9 | Cache strategy | **Read-through cache, TTL ~10-15 min, per (course,date)** | CHOSEN — one decision that simultaneously (a) makes search fast, (b) enforces the polite rate limit / avoids IP bans, (c) gives the watcher its prior-state to diff. |
| #10 | Stateful vs stateless | **STATEFUL** (snapshot store) | FORCED by the watcher — detecting "new slot appeared / slot cancelled" requires remembering the previous snapshot to diff against. |

## Module decomposition locked (prevents a god-module)
Three modules, not one blob. This is the primary guidance for DECOMPOSITION:
1. **Poll+Store layer** — owns fetching (fans out across the 4 adapters on a schedule), the polite-rate policy (xah), the cache, and snapshot retention. The only writer of availability state.
2. **Search/Merge/Rank layer** — reads the store, merges across courses into one time-sorted view, applies the user's filters (day/time window/players/holes) and ranking ("good times"). Produces the UI's data. Stateless over the store.
3. **Watcher/Alert layer** (fast-follow) — reads consecutive snapshots, diffs them against saved rules, emits alerts. Depends on the store existing; does NOT re-poll.
The 4 adapters sit under layer 1 behind the `AvailabilityAdapter` seam.

## Smell risks named + the invariant that prevents each
- **God-module (Bloaters family)** — plain terms: one file that grows to own everything (fetch + parse + cache + merge + rank + alert + notify) until no one can change it safely. Prevented by the 3-module split above.
- **Divergent Change / Feature Envy (Change-Preventers / Couplers)** — plain terms: the aggregator starts special-casing individual backends ("if Tee-On, fix the timezone…"), so a change to one backend forces edits in shared code. Prevented by invariant I2 — backend quirks never leak past the adapter.
- **Shotgun Surgery (Change-Preventers)** — plain terms: one logical change ("add a course") forces edits in many files. Prevented by config-driven courses: adding a course on an existing backend = add one `courseRef` config entry; only a genuinely NEW backend needs a new adapter (bounded, expected).
- **Seam check (DEPTH):** `listSlots(...) -> Slot[]` hides HTTP, auth, Cloudflare handling, parsing, tz, pagination behind a 3-arg call — a DEEP interface, passes the deletion test. The seam is REAL (4 adapters), not speculative. No depth-lean finding.

## Degradation contract (locked — invariant A3)
Any backend we cannot scrape (EZLinks is the likely case) degrades to **deep-link-only**: the course still appears in results with a "check times ->" link to its own booking page and a `stale/unscraped` flag, rather than vanishing. Coverage is never silently dropped; it's visibly degraded.

## Top risks carried from research (mitigations locked)
- **R1 — EZLinks (GolfNow/NBC) anti-bot** blocks 2 courses (25%). Mitigation: sequence EZLinks LAST; deep-link-only fallback (A3) means worst case is 75% scraped + 100% surfaced. Not single-point-fatal.
- **R2 — SPA/devtools dependency** for EZLinks/Chronogolf/LinkLine: each adapter is BLOCKED until one real-browser capture of its endpoint+auth+schema (open-question tee-times-azn). A per-backend capture spike precedes each adapter in DECOMPOSITION.
- **R3 — brittle scraped contracts**: golden-fixture parser tests per adapter; a health signal that distinguishes "0 real slots" from "adapter broke" (invariant I1); honest-UA + polite rate (xah) so IP-bans don't masquerade as outages.

## VETOABLE decisions surfaced to the user (not laundered)
- **A2 — sharing shape:** assumed ONE shared app + shared course list, NO auth, NO login anywhere. If the user wants per-person saved searches/alert rules, layer 3 gains a lightweight user model; layers 1-2 are unaffected. (Asked; user was away.)
- **Watcher freshness (~15 min, not instant):** a forced consequence of #7 + polite rate, but the user should confirm this meets the "grab scarce slots first" expectation before we invest in the watcher.
- **EZLinks effort ceiling:** how hard to fight Cloudflare/partner-API for EZLinks vs accept deep-link-only — deferred to that adapter's build (sequenced last), decided then.

## Architecture status: READY FOR EXECUTION pending user sign-off of the RESEARCH+ARCHITECTURE gate and the A2/watcher-freshness confirmations. No implementation beads authored (interlock correctly holds backlog until DECOMPOSITION).

description:

WHAT (after channel chosen): implement NotificationSink for the chosen channel (send(alert) formats a message with course, date, time, spots, and a deep booking link). Config for recipient(s)/webhook.

INTEGRATION (test/watcher/sink.integration.test.ts): against the channel's sandbox/mock server (e.g. a local webhook receiver) — a real send round-trip, NOT a mocked client.
ACCEPTANCE: a real alert delivered to the chosen channel's test endpoint.
